perf.regime: Memory-bound tall-skinny fused GEMM (huge K, tiny N): one program per (split, row-tile) fuses the squared-sum into the same A load. ~120x over naive fp32 F.linear on gfx942 decode shapes (issue #36), in part because the naive fp32 dense-GEMM path is slow on this torch/rocm stack. Perf unmeasured without a GPU.

notes: Portable Triton. The split-K parallelization is for occupancy at small T; its correctness contract is the SUM invariant (gemm_out_mul.sum(0) == F.linear(a,fn)), so the mandatory sweep pins n_splits=1 where the per-split tensor equals the sum and is element-wise comparable. Internal BLOCK_M/BLOCK_K/BLOCK_N autotune tiles are not exposed at the entry callable (hc_prenorm_gemm_triton only takes n_splits), so they are not declared as knobs here. wave_size not load-bearing at this metadata tier.
